element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Can be charged to fire one powerful shot
Can be pointed in straight and diagonal directions; has extended range when charged
Launches ricocheting spheres that multiply when fully charged
Summons three-headed dragon to destroy all enemies and fired shots onscreen

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully clearing screen with dragon summon
typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters (implied: Zareth disciples and demons)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based action with power-up driven rhythm modulation; tension builds through enemy density and attack patterns, releases through weapon acquisition and screen-clear mechanics

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction via basic energy ball; single attack type establishes foundational player control
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon variation via power-ups increases tactical complexity; enemy density and pattern complexity escalates
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multiple demon and disciple types; culmination in confrontation with Zareth and resurrection of Zao
notable_spikes: Transitions between weapon types (learning curve for directional flame and ricochet patterns)

notable_level_patterns.description: Three distinct weapon systems introduced sequentially via power-up pickups rather than level-based progression; player agency in spell selection creates tactical variation within linear progression
Three-headed dragon summon acts as panic button / tension relief valve; limited resource creates risk-reward decision points during intense enemy waves
All weapons support charge mechanics (single shot, directional flame, multiplying spheres); charging creates vulnerability windows, adding tactical depth to aggression vs. defense tradeoff
